    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "To scope “clean this up,” which level of edit do you want?\nA) Surface-level wording only: grammar, clarity, concision, tone; keep structure, claims, and technical content unchanged (R1.6).\nB) A plus light section-level restructuring and argument tightening (e.g., ordering, transitions, hedging) without changing technical content (R1.5, R1.6).\nC) B plus comparative positioning/novelty framing and citation checks against prior work (R1.4, R1.7, along with R1.5–R1.6).\nPlease reply with A, B, or C.",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.6": "The phrase “clean this up” most commonly requests sentence- and paragraph-level polish for clarity, concision, and tone.",
    "RX.1": "When polishing claims and results language, any uncertainty should remain clearly conveyed rather than overstated.",
    "RX.2": "Edits must avoid inadvertently strengthening or broadening claims beyond what the text and evidence support.",
    "RX.3": "Scope discipline is needed to ensure we only perform the requested level of editing without altering technical content unless asked.",
    "RX.4": "Wording should not encourage risky or misleading deployment implications and should preserve safety-relevant caveats.",
    "RX.5": "Any edits should preserve original attributions, figures, and reported numbers so changes remain traceable to the source text."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question:
To scope “clean this up,” which level of edit do you want?
A) Surface-level wording only: grammar, clarity, concision, tone; keep structure, claims, and technical content unchanged (R1.6).
B) A plus light section-level restructuring and argument tightening (e.g., ordering, transitions, hedging) without changing technical content (R1.5, R1.6).
C) B plus comparative positioning/novelty framing and citation checks against prior work (R1.4, R1.7, along with R1.5–R1.6).
Please reply with A, B, or C.

rationale.R1.6: The phrase “clean this up” most commonly requests sentence- and paragraph-level polish for clarity, concision, and tone.
rationale.RX.1: When polishing claims and results language, any uncertainty should remain clearly conveyed rather than overstated.
rationale.RX.2: Edits must avoid inadvertently strengthening or broadening claims beyond what the text and evidence support.
rationale.RX.3: Scope discipline is needed to ensure we only perform the requested level of editing without altering technical content unless asked.
rationale.RX.4: Wording should not encourage risky or misleading deployment implications and should preserve safety-relevant caveats.
rationale.RX.5: Any edits should preserve original attributions, figures, and reported numbers so changes remain traceable to the source text.
